name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (Urban)
description: Provides financial assistance to urban poor for construction or purchase of pucca houses. Beneficiaries get interest subsidy on home loans.
benefits: Interest subsidy up to 6.5% on home loans
Subsidy amount up to ₹2.67 lakh
Applicable for loans up to ₹6 lakh (EWS/LIG category)
documents: Affidavit of not owning a pucca house
applicationProcess: Visit the official PMAY website or nearest Common Service Centre (CSC)
Fill in the application form with personal and income details
Upload required documents
Submit and note the application reference number
Track status on pmaymis.gov.in

name: National Scholarship Portal – Pre-Matric Scholarship (SC Students)
description: Financial assistance to SC students studying in Class 9 and 10 to help them continue their education without financial burden.
benefits: ₹150/month for day scholars
₹350/month for hostellers
Annual maintenance allowance
applicationProcess: Register on scholarships.gov.in
Select the pre-matric SC scholarship scheme
Fill personal, academic, and bank details
Upload documents
Submit before the deadline (usually October–November)

name: Post-Matric Scholarship for OBC Students
description: Scholarship for OBC students pursuing post-matric education (Class 11 onwards) to reduce dropout rates and promote higher education.
benefits: Maintenance allowance per month (varies by course level)
Reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees
Additional allowances for students with disabilities
applicationProcess: Visit scholarships.gov.in and register
Select Post-Matric Scholarship for OBC
Fill in course, institution, and income details
Upload supporting documents
Submit and track application

name: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana
description: National financial inclusion mission to ensure access to financial services like banking, savings, credit, insurance, and pension for all Indians.
benefits: Zero balance savings account
RuPay debit card with ₹2 lakh accident insurance
Overdraft facility up to ₹10,000 after 6 months
Life insurance cover of ₹30,000
Direct benefit transfer (DBT) eligible
applicationProcess: Visit any bank branch or Business Correspondent (BC) outlet
Fill the PMJDY account opening form
Submit Aadhaar or alternate KYC document
Account is opened instantly

name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi
description: Income support scheme providing ₹6,000 per year to all farmer families to supplement their financial needs.
benefits: ₹6,000 per year in 3 equal instalments of ₹2,000
Directly transferred to bank account via DBT
applicationProcess: Visit pmkisan.gov.in or nearest CSC
Register as a new farmer with land and bank details
Aadhaar verification is mandatory
Instalment status can be checked on the portal

name: Ayushman Bharat – Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana
description: World's largest government-funded health insurance scheme providing coverage of ₹5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary hospitalisation.
benefits: Health cover up to ₹5 lakh per family per year
Covers pre and post hospitalisation expenses
Cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals
No cap on family size or age
applicationProcess: Check eligibility at pmjay.gov.in using mobile number or Ration Card
Visit any empanelled hospital for cashless treatment
Show Aadhaar at the hospital's Ayushman helpdesk
No premium payment required

name: Haryana State Merit Scholarship
description: Scholarship by Haryana government for meritorious students from economically weaker sections studying in Class 1 to post-graduation.
benefits: ₹200 to ₹1,000 per month depending on class level
Additional merit awards for top performers
applicationProcess: Visit haryanascbc.gov.in or scholarships.gov.in
Register and select the relevant state scholarship
Fill in academic and income details
Upload documents and submit before deadline

name: Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana
description: Provides loans up to ₹10 lakh to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises for income-generating activities.
benefits: Shishu: Loans up to ₹50,000
Kishore: Loans from ₹50,001 to ₹5 lakh
Tarun: Loans from ₹5 lakh to ₹10 lakh
No collateral required for Shishu category
MUDRA RuPay card for working capital
applicationProcess: Approach any bank, MFI, or NBFC offering MUDRA loans
Fill MUDRA loan application form
Submit business details and required documents
Loan is sanctioned based on creditworthiness and business plan
Track on mudra.org.in

name: Beti Bachao Beti Padhao – Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana
description: Small deposit savings scheme for the girl child providing tax-free returns and financial security for education and marriage expenses.

eligibility.special_note: Account must be opened before the girl turns 10. Parent/guardian opens the account.

benefits: High interest rate (currently ~8.2% p.a., revised quarterly)
Tax exemption under Section 80C
Partial withdrawal allowed at age 18 for education
Account matures when girl turns 21
applicationProcess: Visit any Post Office or authorised bank branch
Fill the SSY account opening form
Minimum deposit of ₹250 to open the account
Maximum deposit of ₹1.5 lakh per year
Passbook issued for tracking

name: Atal Pension Yojana
description: Pension scheme focused on unorganised sector workers guaranteeing a minimum monthly pension of ₹1,000 to ₹5,000 after age 60.
benefits: Guaranteed pension of ₹1,000 / ₹2,000 / ₹3,000 / ₹4,000 / ₹5,000 per month at age 60
Government co-contribution of 50% for eligible subscribers (early joiners)
Spouse continues pension after subscriber's death
Nominee receives corpus on death of both
applicationProcess: Visit your bank branch or use net banking
Fill APY registration form
Choose desired pension amount (₹1K–₹5K)
Auto-debit set up for monthly contributions
Track on npscra.nsdl.co.in

name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana
description: Accidental death and disability insurance scheme offering coverage of ₹2 lakh at a premium of just ₹20 per year for bank account holders.
benefits: ₹2 lakh on accidental death or permanent total disability
₹1 lakh on permanent partial disability
Annual premium of only ₹20 (auto-debited from bank account)
applicationProcess: Visit your bank branch, net banking portal, or Jan Dhan account
Fill PMSBY enrollment form or enroll via SMS/mobile banking
Auto-debit of ₹20 is set up from your account
Policy renews automatically every year on 1st June

name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana
description: Life insurance scheme offering ₹2 lakh death cover for any reason at an annual premium of ₹436 for bank account holders.
benefits: ₹2 lakh life insurance cover for death due to any reason
Annual premium of ₹436 (auto-debited from bank account)
No medical examination required
applicationProcess: Visit your bank branch or enroll via net banking / mobile banking
Fill PMJJBY enrollment-cum-auto-debit form
Annual premium of ₹436 is auto-debited on 1st June each year
Nominee receives ₹2 lakh on policyholder's death

name: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act
description: Guarantees 100 days of wage employment per financial year to every rural household whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work.
benefits: 100 days of guaranteed wage employment per year
Minimum wages as per state schedule (₹200–₹350/day)
Unemployment allowance if work not provided within 15 days
Work provided within 5 km of residence
documents: Bank Account or Post Office Account
applicationProcess: Register at your local Gram Panchayat office
Apply for a Job Card (free of cost)
Submit work application to Gram Panchayat when needed
Work must be assigned within 15 days of application
Wages paid directly to bank/post office account within 15 days of work

name: Stand-Up India Scheme
description: Facilitates bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 crore to at least one SC/ST borrower and one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up greenfield enterprises.

eligibility.special_note: Eligible if SC/ST (any gender) OR female (any caste). Business must be a greenfield (new) enterprise.

benefits: Composite loan between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 crore
Covers 75% of project cost
Repayment period up to 7 years
Moratorium period up to 18 months
documents: Proof of no existing default with any bank
applicationProcess: Apply online at standupmitra.in or visit any Scheduled Commercial Bank branch
Register and fill in business and personal details
Submit project report and financial documents
Bank processes loan after due diligence
Loan disbursed to business account

name: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana
description: Flagship skill development scheme enabling Indian youth to take industry-relevant skill training and earn a government-recognised certificate.
benefits: Free skill training across 300+ job roles
Government-recognised National Skills Qualification Framework (NSQF) certificate
Monetary reward of ₹8,000 on successful completion
Placement assistance after training
applicationProcess: Visit skillindia.gov.in or the PMKVY portal
Find a training centre near you
Register with personal and educational details
Complete the selected skill course (duration varies)
Appear for assessment and receive certificate

name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (Gramin)
description: Provides financial assistance to rural BPL households to construct a pucca house with basic amenities.
benefits: ₹1.20 lakh assistance in plain areas
₹1.30 lakh assistance in hilly/difficult areas
Additional ₹12,000 for toilet construction under Swachh Bharat Mission
90 days of unskilled labour under MGNREGA
applicationProcess: Beneficiaries are selected based on SECC-2011 data by Gram Sabha
Verify name on awaassoft.nic.in
Register at Gram Panchayat or Block Development Office
Funds released in instalments upon construction progress
Geo-tagged photos submitted at each stage

name: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana
description: Provides free LPG connections to women from BPL households to replace unclean cooking fuels and improve health and environment.
benefits: Free LPG connection with first refill and stove
EMI facility for stove and first refill cost
Subsidy on subsequent refills via DBT
Improved indoor air quality and women's health
documents: Self-declaration of not having LPG connection
applicationProcess: Visit nearest LPG distributor (HP, Bharat, or Indane Gas)
Fill the KYC form for Ujjwala Yojana
Submit BPL card and Aadhaar
Connection released after verification
Subsidy credited directly to bank account

name: Haryana Mukhyamantri Vivah Shagun Yojana
description: Financial assistance provided by Haryana government to families at the time of marriage of their daughters, especially from SC/ST, BPL, and widow families.

eligibility.special_note: Applicable at time of marriage. Bride must be 18+, groom must be 21+.

benefits: ₹71,000 for SC/ST/BPL widow families
₹31,000 for SC/ST general families
₹21,000 for BPL and destitute women
₹11,000 for other eligible categories
applicationProcess: Apply online at saralharyana.gov.in before or within 6 months of marriage
Fill personal and marriage details
Upload required documents
Application verified by local authorities
Amount credited to bride's bank account

name: Scholarship for Students with Disabilities (National)
description: Financial assistance to students with benchmark disabilities pursuing education from Class 9 to post-graduation level.
benefits: ₹500 to ₹2,000 per month maintenance allowance
Book allowance and transport allowance
Additional allowances for assistive devices
applicationProcess: Register on scholarships.gov.in
Select the scholarship for students with disabilities
Enter disability certificate details and upload documents
Submit before the annual deadline
Renewal required every year with updated marksheet

name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme
description: Monthly pension for destitute senior citizens aged 60 and above from BPL households under the National Social Assistance Programme.
benefits: ₹200/month for ages 60–79 (central contribution)
₹500/month for ages 80 and above
States may add additional top-up (e.g. Haryana gives ₹2,500/month total)
documents: Age Proof (Birth Certificate / School Certificate / Voter ID)
applicationProcess: Visit your local Gram Panchayat (rural) or Municipal Office (urban)
Fill the IGNOAPS application form
Submit age proof, BPL card, and Aadhaar
Application forwarded to District Social Welfare Officer
Pension disbursed monthly to bank account after approval
